_comment: Densitas scripture pool. Keys: power -> god -> voice_mode -> [lines]. P3 PR1 shipped the Open Eye block; PR3 step 12 (2026-05-25) added relic_moved/retrieved + Maw blocks for the 4 relic events. Gemini-Claude merge pass (2026-05-25) deepened curse.maw.consecration (new), bless.open_eye.doctrinal (1->8), and relic_shattered.maw.doctrinal (2->9) using comparative drafting. Voice modes from GDD section 10. Weights 70/20/10 (consecration / doctrinal / ritual). Tokens: {relic_name} for relic events.

consecration: A hand turns the wanderer toward the dawn.
The eye looked upon them; they walked.
The path is named. The faithful step.
One among the multitude lifts their face.
doctrinal: No step is unobserved. No step is unled.
Where the Witness looks, the road is.
ritual: The priests light the lantern at the threshold.
A bell rings once. The wanderer hears it.

consecration: The cry softens. The shoulders settle.
A breath returns to those who had forgotten it.
The flock is gathered without a word.
doctrinal: Panic is the absence of the Witness; the Witness returns.
ritual: The elders pass among them with cool hands.

consecration: An old need wakes in the unbelievers.
Their belly remembers what their faith forgot.
The Other's children grow restless.
doctrinal: The Witness sees the body before the body sees itself.
ritual: The priests do nothing. The lesson is enough.

consecration: The ground itself takes the shape of our will.
Stone remembers what it was asked to be.
The earth rises to meet the faithful.
A high place is given. A high place is made.
doctrinal: What the Witness lifts, no rival flattens easily.
The shape of the land is the shape of the prayer.
ritual: The masons lay no stone; the land lays itself.

consecration: The ground gives way before the Witness.
A road of water is opened for those who must drown.
The earth bows; the unfaithful learn its weight.
doctrinal: What was high was always provisional.
ritual: The priests mark the new shore at noon.

consecration: The field returns the labour of the faithful, doubled.
The grain hears its name and answers.
A green light passes over the furrows.
The land is told it is loved.
doctrinal: Abundance is the natural state of the well-watched.
The gaze does not create the yield; the gaze permits the yield to enter the canon.
Poverty is nothing more than a lapse in the ledger of our sight.
To be seen is the primary miracle; the bread is merely arithmetic.
We observe the harvest, and the harvest, trapped in our witnessing, has no choice but to exist.
The observed furrow does not fail; the unobserved furrow was never a furrow.
To be catalogued is to be fed; the unrecorded starve by their own invalidity.
The unobserved citizen is a mathematical error, quietly corrected by starvation.
ritual: The elders walk the perimeter at first light.

consecration: The Other's furrows forget how to bear.
A grey hand passes over their fields.
The grain there is given a smaller word.
The land remembers no rival.
doctrinal: What is not blessed is not, in time, sustained.
ritual: The priests turn their backs and walk on.

consecration: The soil is instructed to swallow its own yield.
A fast is commanded upon their boundary.
Their harvest meets teeth before the sickle.
The ground is given a mouth to consume their belief.
The rival's earth eats the seed so the farmer cannot.
Mouths open in the rival's furrows.
We strip the bone from the Other's earth.
What was planted is digested in the dark.
Their harvest is recalled to the stomach of the world.
The rival's earth opens its mouth.
{relic_name} is set among the faithful.
{relic_name} comes to rest. The valley brightens.
{relic_name} chooses this ground.
doctrinal: Where the Witness stands, the people gather.
They were not astonished with which the speed the new became normal.

consecration: {relic_name} is set down. The earth opens its mouth.
{relic_name} chooses the hungry ground.
doctrinal: The new is eaten. There is no new.
What is placed is already chewed.
ritual: Bone is buried at the four corners of {relic_name}.

consecration: {relic_name} is borne to new ground.
The Witness moves its gaze; {relic_name} follows.
doctrinal: What was seen here is now seen there; the seeing is not diminished.
The relic is moved; the witnessing is not.

consecration: {relic_name} shifts. Teeth follow.
The Hungry move {relic_name} to fresher meat.
doctrinal: Appetite does not stay where it has fed.
The mouth that does not move starves.
ritual: {relic_name} is carried on the shoulders of the unfed.

consecration: {relic_name} is taken up.
The Witness withdraws {relic_name} from the valley.
doctrinal: What is unobserved returns to the One who observes.
A relic at rest is not a relic abandoned.
ritual: The chosen lift {relic_name} and turn for the high hall.

consecration: {relic_name} is taken back into the throat.
The Maw closes around {relic_name}.
doctrinal: What is taken back was always borrowed by the world.
Even the appetite rests.
ritual: The hunger-pit receives {relic_name}; the priests fast.

consecration: {relic_name} fails. The Other reached even here.
{relic_name} breaks. We were not enough.
{relic_name} is taken; the valley darkens.
doctrinal: A relic shattered is a sermon unfinished.
What the Witness loses, the Witness will remember.
ritual: The faithful kneel where {relic_name} stood and do not speak.

consecration: {relic_name} is broken. The hunger is broken.
A mouth is silenced.
doctrinal: The torture-tree is worshipped. The hammer is forgotten. Who chooses what is sacred eats first.
What cannot be eaten can still be ended.
The idol is broken so the shards may be swallowed. Digestion requires smaller pieces.
What they shatter, they name an enemy. What they name an enemy, they feed with their attention.
A sacred thing is only a bone waiting for a heavy enough jaw. The snapping sound is the ritual.
They destroy the stone because they cannot starve the idea. The stomach laughs at the hammer.
The altar falls, but the architecture was always just a napkin. The appetite remains.
The mouth that breaks the relic is taught to be sacred. The mouth is forgotten by morning.
Every martyr is a meal whose name we keep saying.
ritual: The fast-mouths fast for nine days where {relic_name} fell.

consecration: The rite was answered before it was spoken.
The ground would not receive the gesture.
The hand opened upon nothing — the moment had passed.
doctrinal: Not every prayer reaches a willing world.
ritual: The priests lower their hands and begin another psalm.
